---
title: "Bob Week"
output: 
  html_document: 
    theme: cosmo
    css: style.css
---

<!--<img src="images/users/meprof.jpg" height="150" width="150" border="0"/>-->

I am a PhD candidate at the University of Idaho working with professor [Scott Nuismer](http://www.webpages.uidaho.edu/~snuismer/Nuismer_Lab/). My degree program is [Bioinformatics and Computational Biology](http://www.uidaho.edu/sci/bcb) (BCB) which is housed within [The Institute for Bioinformatics and Evolutionary Studies](http://www.ibest.uidaho.edu/) (IBEST).

My interests are in extending coevolutionary theory to the community context. Currently I am focused on doing this entirely in terms of phenotypic evolution, that is, deriving all of my models from a quantitative genetic (QG) foundation. Hence, I live in the world of microevolution. However, I have begun to think about larger spatial and temporal scales and thus how to marry QG with phylogenetics and metacommunity ecology.

My CV is available [here](files/cv.pdf).

### Updates

  * I have recently finished developing a method for measuring pairwise coevolution in nature which will hopefully be published soon.
